mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate signals easier discounting and higher risk appetite, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS is falling over the prior 3 months, or rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and slower demand, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative 20-day SPX returns more often than 50%, or if the average forward return after the trigger is non-negative across two consecutive rolling 18-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: A joint acceleration in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broadening payroll income and retail demand, supporting earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-RSAFS growth state does not outperform the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point over 60-day forward SPX returns, or if either variable alone explains the signal as well as the joint state.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO reflects stronger industrial output and utilization, which improves cyclical earnings expectations and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns after the trigger are not positive in at least 52% of cases, or if the signal disappears after controlling for the prior month SPX trend.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation break above the prior 6-month pace forces tighter policy expectations and lower equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation exceeds its prior 12-month average by 0.5 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-break episodes are not followed by below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect vanishes when FEDFUNDS is unchanged.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST signals improving housing demand and easier credit transmission, which supports equity cyclicals and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX returns are not higher than baseline during sustained HOUST uptrends, or if the relationship reverses in more than 40% of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth slows while UNRATE rises, the labor market is weakening in a way that reduces consumer spending and corporate revenue, implying negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor-deterioration state does not produce below-median 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign remains negative even when PAYEMS is accelerating and UNRATE is flat.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when GDP growth is weakening while retail sales remain firm, markets treat the pattern as temporary softness rather than recession, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if weak-GDPC1/firm-RSAFS episodes do not outperform baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect is absent once quarterly GDP revisions are incorporated.
